title: Login with valid credentials
steps: Go to login.html. Enter valid email and password. Click Log In.
expected: Redirects to dashboard. Sidebar shows correct tenant name, role badge, and display name.

title: Login with wrong password
steps: Go to login.html. Enter valid email and a wrong password. Click Log In.
expected: Error message appears below the form. Page does not redirect.

title: Logout
steps: While logged in, click Log Out in the sidebar.
expected: Redirected to login.html. Navigating back to the app redirects to login.html.

title: Session persists across page refresh
steps: Log in. Press F5 to refresh the page.
expected: Dashboard reloads. User is still logged in. Sidebar still shows correct name and role.

title: Forgot password — success message
steps: Go to login.html. Click 'Forgot password?'. Enter your email. Click Send.
expected: Success message appears. No error is shown. (Email delivery requires working SMTP — confirm message display only.)

title: Set Password page — valid invite token shows welcome form
steps: Generate an invite link in Settings → Users. Open the invite URL (format: /set-password.html?invite=<uuid>) in an incognito browser window.
expected: Page shows 'Welcome to Staffing Intelligence' heading, tenant name, role, and the invited email (non-editable). Password and Confirm Password fields are present. 'Set Password & Sign In' button is visible.

title: Set Password page — successful submission redirects to login
steps: On the set-password.html page with a valid invite token, enter a matching password (8+ characters) in both fields. Click 'Set Password & Sign In'.
expected: Shows 'Password set! Redirecting to login…' message. Automatically redirects to login.html within 2 seconds. User can then log in with the new password.

title: KPI cards render with numeric values
steps: Log in and navigate to the Overview tab.
expected: All KPI cards (Available Capacity %, On Bench count, Open Needs count) display numeric values. No card shows blank, NaN, or an error state.

title: Bench KPI drilldown opens modal with consultant list
steps: On the Overview tab, click the On Bench KPI card.
expected: A drilldown modal or panel opens. It lists consultants with 0–10h this week, with their names and current hours.

title: Open Needs KPI drilldown opens needs detail
steps: On the Overview tab, click the Open Needs KPI card.
expected: A drilldown opens showing open needs. Each item shows project, level, and dates.

title: Rolling Off Soon panel lists upcoming rolloffs
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Rolling Off Soon panel.
expected: Consultants whose project assignments end in the next 4 weeks appear, with project name and end date.

title: Overallocated Resources panel shows 46h+ consultants
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Overallocated Resources panel.
expected: Consultants assigned more than 45h/week appear in the list. Each row shows name and current hours.

title: Top Projects panel shows most utilized projects
steps: On the Overview tab, view the Top Projects panel.
expected: A list of projects appears ordered by total hours this week. Each row shows project name and hours.

title: Chart tooltips appear on hover
steps: On the Overview tab, hover over bars or segments in any visible chart.
expected: A tooltip appears showing relevant data (label and value). Tooltip disappears when cursor moves away.

title: Alt+R refreshes dashboard without full page reload
steps: On the Overview tab, press Alt+R.
expected: KPIs and panels reload. No browser-level page refresh occurs (address bar stays the same, tab does not blink).

title: Drilldown modals — expand/collapse works correctly
steps: Click any KPI card that opens a drilldown modal. Observe initial state, then use the Expand/Collapse All button and individual section toggles.
expected: All consultant group sections open expanded by default (by design). An Expand/Collapse All button is present above the rows. Clicking it collapses all sections; clicking again expands all. Individual section headers also toggle their own section.

title: Heatmap loads correct consultants for tenant
steps: Navigate to the Resource Allocation tab.
expected: Heatmap shows only consultants belonging to your tenant. No consultants from other tenants appear.

title: Color coding — bench, partial, utilized, overallocated
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, observe cell colors across the heatmap.
expected: Red cells = 0–10h (bench). Yellow = 11–44h (partial). Green = 45h (fully utilized). Orange = 46h+ (overallocated).

title: Expand consultant row shows project sub-rows
steps: Click the chevron next to a consultant name to expand their row.
expected: Row expands to show one sub-row per project, with individual hours per week for each project.

title: Expand All and Collapse All buttons work
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, click Expand All. Then click Collapse All.
expected: Expand All opens sub-rows for all consultants. Collapse All closes them all. Both actions complete without error.

title: Inline cell editing — click, type, blur to save
steps: Click a week cell in the heatmap. Type a new number (e.g. 40). Click elsewhere on the page.
expected: Cell saves the new value. Cell color updates to reflect the new utilization band. No page reload occurs.

title: Save confirmation appears after cell edit
steps: Edit a heatmap cell (click, type, blur).
expected: A brief save confirmation indicator (toast or status message) appears after the save, then disappears.

title: Keyboard nav — Tab moves between cells horizontally
steps: Click a heatmap cell. Press Tab repeatedly.
expected: Focus moves to the next cell in the row. At the end of a row, focus wraps to the next row. Each focused cell is editable.

title: Keyboard nav — Enter saves and moves down to next row
steps: Click a heatmap cell. Type a value. Press Enter.
expected: Value is saved. Focus moves to the same column in the next row (skipping consultants with no project sub-rows).

title: Quick Fill uses API path, not DOM
steps: Expand a consultant's project sub-row. Use the Quick Fill bar to enter hours for a date range. Apply.
expected: Hours are written to the correct cells. Open the browser DevTools Network tab first and confirm a POST to /api/save-staffing is made. No DOM cell matching should occur.

title: Consultant name click opens profile modal
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, click a consultant's name.
expected: A profile modal opens showing the consultant's details (name, level, skills, location). Modal is not editable for PM and executive roles.

title: Virtual scroll — smooth performance with many rows
steps: On the Resource Allocation tab, scroll rapidly up and down through all consultants.
expected: Scrolling is smooth. Rows render correctly without blank gaps or missing data.

title: Heatmap is read-only for project manager and executive
steps: Log in as project manager or executive. Go to Resource Allocation. Click a week cell.
expected: Cell does not become editable. No input field appears. Quick Fill bar is not shown.

title: Needs tab loads with open needs listed
steps: Navigate to the Open Needs tab.
expected: A list of open staffing needs loads. Each row shows project, level, skills, hours/week, and dates.

title: Donut chart renders with client segments
steps: On the Open Needs tab, view the donut chart at the top.
expected: A donut chart renders with one colored segment per client. A legend shows client names. Segment sizes reflect open need counts per client.

title: Donut segment hover tooltip shows client name and count
steps: On the Open Needs tab, hover the mouse cursor over a donut chart segment.
expected: A tooltip appears showing the client name as the title and 'X open needs' as the body. Tooltip disappears when cursor moves away.

title: Donut click filters needs list by client
steps: Click a segment on the Open Needs donut chart.
expected: The needs list below filters to show only that client's needs. All other clients' needs are hidden.

title: Donut click again clears filter
steps: Click a donut segment to filter. Then click the same segment again.
expected: Filter clears. All clients' needs reappear in the list.

title: Client header grouping with open-need count badge
steps: On the Open Needs tab, view the needs list.
expected: Needs are grouped under client header rows. Each client header shows a count badge indicating how many open needs belong to that client.

title: Expand All and Collapse All in needs list
steps: On the Open Needs tab, click Expand All. Verify all need rows are visible. Then click Collapse All.
expected: Expand All reveals all need rows under every client. Collapse All collapses all client groups, hiding individual need rows.

title: Urgency badges — Urgent, Soon, Planned
steps: On the Open Needs tab, observe the badge on each need row.
expected: Needs starting within 2 weeks show 'Urgent' in red. Needs 2–4 weeks out show 'Soon' in yellow. Needs 4+ weeks out show 'Planned' in green.

title: Needs sorted by urgency (soonest start first)
steps: On the Open Needs tab, view the order of need rows within any client group.
expected: Needs are sorted with the soonest start date first (most urgent at the top of each group).

title: Add Need — step 1 project selection
steps: Click the Add Need button. Observe step 1 of the modal.
expected: A modal opens showing a project selection list or dropdown. A Next button is visible. The form does not skip to step 2.

title: Add Need — step 2 shows bulk row form with project and client badge
steps: In the Add Need modal, select a project and click Next.
expected: Step 2 opens showing the selected project name and client name at the top. A bulk form appears with shared start/end date fields and at least one row containing Level, Skills, Hrs/Wk, and Qty fields.

title: Add Need — level dropdown reversed (Partner/MD first)
steps: In the Add Need modal step 2, open the Level dropdown on any row.
expected: Levels appear in descending order (Partner/MD at the top, Analyst at the bottom).

title: Add Need — skills multi-select per row with pill tags
steps: In the Add Need modal step 2, click the Skills selector on a row. Toggle two or more skills.
expected: Selected skills appear as pill tags in the trigger. If more than one is selected, the trigger shows '1 skill name + N more'. Clicking a skill again removes it.

title: Add Need — Qty field creates N independent need records
steps: In the Add Need modal step 2, set Qty to 3 on a row. Fill in all other fields. Click Create Needs.
expected: A progress counter shows 'Creating… (X/3)'. After completion, 3 separate need records appear in the needs list with identical level/skills/hours/dates.

title: Add Need — add and remove extra rows
steps: In the Add Need modal step 2, click '+ Add another need' to add a second row. Then click the ✕ button on that row.
expected: A second row appears when adding. Clicking ✕ removes the row. The ✕ button on the only remaining row is hidden (cannot remove last row).

title: Add Need — per-row validation shows row number in error
steps: In the Add Need modal step 2, leave one row's Level field blank. Click Create Needs.
expected: An error message appears indicating which row number has the issue (e.g. 'Row 2: Level is required'). No needs are created until all rows are valid.

title: Edit Need — level dropdown reversed
steps: Click Edit on an existing need. Open the Level dropdown in the edit modal.
expected: Levels appear in descending order (Partner/MD first, Analyst last), same as in the Add Need modal.

title: Close need as met
steps: On an open need row, click the '✓ Met' button (visible to admin and resource_manager only). An in-app confirmation modal appears. Click 'Close as Met' to confirm.
expected: Need disappears from the open needs list. Donut chart updates to reflect the reduced count.

title: Close need as abandoned
steps: On an open need row, click the '✗ Abandon' button (visible to admin and resource_manager only). An in-app confirmation modal appears. Click 'Abandon' to confirm.
expected: Need disappears from the open needs list. Donut chart updates to reflect the reduced count.

title: Find Candidates — eligible consultants shown with match badges
steps: Click Find Candidates (or the AI icon) on an open need.
expected: A modal opens listing consultants who match the need's level and skills. Each candidate shows available hours and a colored match badge (green ≥100%, yellow 50–99%, coral <50% of hours needed).

title: Bulk assign consultants to need
steps: Open candidates for a need. Select one or more consultants by checking their rows. Click Assign.
expected: Consultants are assigned to the need's project for the need's date range. Heatmap reflects the new assignments.

title: Auto-close when need fully staffed after bulk assign
steps: Assign enough consultants to a need so that the total assigned hours meets or exceeds the need's required hours.
expected: Need automatically closes with reason 'met'. It disappears from the open needs list without a manual close action.

title: Skill matching uses full skill set (allSkillSets)
steps: Find a need that requires a specific skill. Open candidates for that need and observe which consultants appear.
expected: Consultants who have the required skill anywhere in their full skill set (allSkillSets array) appear as candidates. There is no concept of primary vs. secondary skill — all skills are equal for matching purposes.

title: Suggested questions load on tab open
steps: Click the Ask Claude tab.
expected: 3–5 suggested question chips appear within a few seconds. They are clickable and insert the question text into the input.

title: Free-form question returns a relevant answer
steps: On the Ask Claude tab, type 'Who is currently on the bench?' and press Enter or click Ask.
expected: Claude responds with a formatted answer referencing consultants from your tenant. Response appears in the chat area.

title: Response renders as formatted markdown
steps: Ask Claude a question that typically returns a list or table (e.g. 'List all projects').
expected: Response is rendered with markdown formatting — headers, bullet lists, or tables are displayed visually, not as raw markdown text.

title: Follow-up question context is preserved
steps: Ask 'Who has the most availability this week?' Then ask a follow-up: 'What projects are they on?'
expected: The follow-up response references the specific consultant mentioned in the prior answer. Both the question and answer pairs are visible in the chat history.

title: Empty question shows error, no API call made
steps: On the Ask Claude tab, leave the input blank and click Ask (or press Enter).
expected: An error or warning message appears indicating a question is required. No loading spinner appears and no API call is made.

title: Response references only current tenant's data
steps: Ask Claude 'List all consultants'. Note the names. Log in as admin of a different tenant and ask the same question.
expected: Each tenant sees only their own consultants in Claude's response. No cross-tenant data appears.

title: Consultants panel loads for admin and resource manager
steps: Navigate to Settings. Observe which sub-panel is shown first.
expected: Consultants panel is the default. A list of all active consultants loads with name, level, location, and status columns.

title: Edit consultant name and location
steps: In Settings → Consultants, click a consultant row to open their profile. Change the name or location. Click Save.
expected: Changes save without error. Updated name or location is reflected in the consultant list and heatmap.

title: Add skill to consultant profile
steps: Open a consultant profile in Settings → Consultants. Use the skills selector to add a new skill. Save.
expected: Skill appears in the consultant's skill tags. The skill is considered in candidate matching from this point forward.

title: Remove skill from consultant profile
steps: Open a consultant profile. Click the ✕ on an existing skill chip to remove it. Save.
expected: Skill is removed from the consultant's profile and saved. It no longer appears on their profile.

title: Billing rate fields visible to admin and resource manager
steps: Open a consultant profile in Settings → Consultants.
expected: Bill Rate and Cost Rate fields are visible and show numeric values.

title: Linked User Account dropdown visible to admin only
steps: Log in as admin. Open a consultant profile in Settings → Consultants. Look for the Linked User Account field.
expected: A 'Linked User Account' dropdown or selector is visible, listing auth users available to link to this consultant.

title: Unsaved changes warning on modal close
steps: Open a consultant profile. Make a change to any field. Without saving, click the X or close button.
expected: A warning message or discard confirmation appears asking whether to save or discard changes. Changes are not lost unless confirmed.

title: Deactivate consultant — removed from heatmap and candidate lists
steps: In Settings → Consultants, click Deactivate on an active consultant. Confirm the action.
expected: Consultant status changes to Inactive. They no longer appear in the heatmap or in candidate matching results.

title: Reactivate consultant — restored to heatmap
steps: In Settings → Consultants, find an inactive consultant. Click Reactivate. Confirm.
expected: Consultant status changes back to Active. They reappear in the heatmap.

title: Users panel is visible to admin only
steps: Log in as admin. Navigate to Settings. Look for a Users sub-panel in the left nav.
expected: A 'Users' nav item is visible. Clicking it shows the user management panel with a list of tenant users.

title: User list shows all tenant users with role and status
steps: In Settings → Users, view the user table.
expected: All users for the current tenant are listed. Each row shows email, role, and status (Active / Deactivated / Pending).

title: Invite user — modal shows copyable link with 7-day TTL
steps: In Settings → Users, click Invite User. Fill in full name, email, and a role. Click Generate Invite Link.
expected: Success modal shows a green checkmark, 'Invite Created' heading, the invited email, a copyable invite URL, and 'valid for 7 days' note. 'Copy Invite Link' button works. Clicking Done closes the modal and refreshes the user list.

title: Change user role
steps: In Settings → Users, click on an active user to open their edit panel. Change the role dropdown to a different role. Save.
expected: User's role updates in the list. A note may indicate the user must log in again for the change to take effect.

title: Deactivate user prevents login
steps: In Settings → Users, click Deactivate on an active user. Confirm.
expected: User's status changes to Deactivated. Attempting to log in as that user fails with an appropriate error.

title: Reactivate deactivated user
steps: In Settings → Users, find a deactivated user. Click Reactivate. Confirm.
expected: User's status changes back to Active. The user can log in again.

title: Pending badge based on last sign-in (never signed in = Pending)
steps: In Settings → Users, invite a new user but do not complete the set-password flow. View the user list.
expected: The newly invited user shows a 'Pending' badge (orange/yellow). Users who have logged in at least once show an 'Active' badge (green).

title: Resend Invite — pending user gets a new link
steps: In Settings → Users, find a user with 'Pending' status. Click Resend Invite.
expected: Modal opens showing 'Invite Link Regenerated' with a new copyable invite URL valid for 7 days. The old invite link is no longer valid.

title: Reset Password — active user gets a password reset link
steps: In Settings → Users, find an Active user. Click Reset Password.
expected: Modal opens showing 'Password Reset Link Created' with a copyable URL. Sharing this URL with the user lets them set a new password via set-password.html.

title: Invite user — duplicate active email shows error
steps: In Settings → Users, try to invite an email address that already belongs to an active user (last_sign_in_at not null).
expected: Error message shown: 'User is already active. Use Reset Password instead.' No duplicate user is created.

title: Set Password page — validation errors shown inline
steps: Open a valid invite URL. First try submitting with passwords that do not match. Then try a password shorter than 8 characters.
expected: Mismatched passwords shows 'Passwords do not match.' Short password shows 'Password must be at least 8 characters.' Form does not submit in either case.

title: Set Password page — expired or invalid token shows error
steps: Navigate to /set-password.html with no token (?invite= missing), or with a fake UUID. Also test revisiting an already-used invite URL after password was set.
expected: Page shows an error heading ('Link Expired' or 'Invalid Link') with a message and a mailto:vaprabhakar@deloitte.com link. No password form is shown.

title: Admin sees all 4 Settings sub-nav items and defaults to Consultants
steps: Log in as admin. Navigate to Settings tab.
expected: Settings sub-nav shows 4 items: Consultants, Users, Sandbox, Account. Consultants panel is active by default.

title: Project Manager sees only Account sub-nav, defaults to Account
steps: Log in as pm_test@test.com. Navigate to Settings tab.
expected: Settings sub-nav shows only Account. Consultants, Users, and Sandbox are not visible. Account panel is active by default showing the Change Password form.

title: Executive sees only Account sub-nav, defaults to Account
steps: Log in as exec_test@test.com. Navigate to Settings tab.
expected: Settings sub-nav shows only Account. Consultants, Users, and Sandbox are not visible. Account panel is active by default showing the Change Password form.

title: Resource Manager sees Consultants + Account, Users/Sandbox hidden
steps: Log in as rm_test@test.com. Navigate to Settings tab.
expected: Settings sub-nav shows Consultants and Account only. Users and Sandbox are not visible. Consultants panel is active by default.

title: Account panel renders Change Password form
steps: Log in with any role. Navigate to Settings → Account.
expected: Account panel shows a 'Change Password' heading, 3 password fields (Current Password, New Password, Confirm New Password), and an 'Update Password' button.

title: Empty fields show inline validation error
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Click Update Password without filling in any fields.
expected: Inline error message appears: 'All fields are required.' No API call is made. Fields remain empty.

title: New password under 8 characters shows validation error
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Fill in Current Password, enter 'abc' as New Password and Confirm Password. Click Update Password.
expected: Inline error: 'New password must be at least 8 characters.' No API call made.

title: Mismatched New and Confirm passwords shows error
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Enter a valid current password, enter 'Password1!' as New Password and 'Password2!' as Confirm Password. Click Update Password.
expected: Inline error: 'Passwords do not match.' No API call made.

title: Wrong current password returns inline error, no session disruption
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Enter an incorrect current password with a valid new password (8+ chars, matching confirm). Click Update Password.
expected: Inline error: 'Current password is incorrect.' No redirect to login. Session stays active. Other parts of the app still work.

title: Correct current password + valid new password succeeds
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Enter your actual current password, a new valid password (8+ chars), and matching confirm. Click Update Password.
expected: All 3 fields are cleared. Fields and button become disabled with 0.5 opacity. A green inline message shows: 'Password updated successfully.' After ~4 seconds, the form re-enables and the message disappears.

title: Log in with new password after successful change
steps: After successfully changing password (chp-10), log out. Log back in using the new password.
expected: Login succeeds with the new password. Old password no longer works.

title: Double-click Update Password button sends only one API call
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Fill in all fields correctly. Double-click Update Password quickly.
expected: Button disables after first click. Only one API call is made. No duplicate password-change requests.

title: Account panel persists across tab switches
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Switch to Overview tab. Switch back to Settings.
expected: Settings opens on the Account panel (not Consultants or Users). The remembered panel state is preserved.

title: Enter key in Current Password field submits the form
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Fill in all 3 password fields. Press Enter while focused on the Current Password field.
expected: Form submits as if Update Password was clicked. Validation runs, API call is made.

title: Enter key in Confirm Password field submits the form
steps: Navigate to Settings → Account. Fill in all 3 password fields. Press Enter while focused on the Confirm Password field.
expected: Form submits as if Update Password was clicked. Validation runs, API call is made.

title: After success, inputs and button are disabled for ~4 seconds
steps: Submit a successful password change. Immediately try to type in any of the 3 fields or click Update Password.
expected: All 3 inputs and the button are disabled (visually dimmed, 50% opacity). Cannot type or submit during the 4-second success window.

title: After 4 seconds, form re-enables and is ready for another change
steps: Submit a successful password change. Wait approximately 4 seconds.
expected: All 3 inputs and the button become enabled again. The green success message disappears. The form is ready to accept another password change.

title: Meridian: no 'Unknown' segment — internal projects show as 'Non-Client'
steps: Log in to the Meridian tenant (vaprabhakar@deloitte.com). Navigate to Open Needs tab. Inspect the donut chart segments.
expected: No segment labelled 'Unknown'. Needs from Pre-Sales Support and Internal Training appear under a segment labelled 'Non-Client'. All other segments show named clients.

title: Meridian: clicking 'Non-Client' segment filters rows to non-client needs
steps: On the Meridian Open Needs tab, click the 'Non-Client' donut segment.
expected: The needs table filters to show only needs belonging to projects without a client (Pre-Sales Support, Internal Training). At least one row is visible. Named-client needs are hidden.

title: Meridian: clicking a named client segment filters rows to that client only
steps: On the Meridian Open Needs tab, click any named client segment (not 'Non-Client') in the donut chart.
expected: The needs table shows only needs for that client. The count of visible rows matches the segment count shown in the donut tooltip.

title: Clicking the active segment again clears the filter
steps: On the Meridian Open Needs tab, click a donut segment to filter. Then click the same segment again.
expected: The filter clears. All open needs are visible again. No segment remains highlighted.

title: Acme Corp donut shows only named client segments — no 'Non-Client' or 'Unknown'
steps: Log in to the Acme Corp tenant. Navigate to Open Needs tab. Inspect the donut chart segments.
expected: All segments show named clients. No 'Non-Client' or 'Unknown' segment appears.

title: Acme Corp: donut click filter works correctly
steps: On the Acme Corp Open Needs tab, click any client segment in the donut chart.
expected: The needs table filters to show only that client's needs. Click the same segment again to clear. All needs reappear.

title: Admin — full access: edit heatmap, close needs, manage users
steps: Log in as admin. Edit a heatmap cell. Close a need. Invite a user in Settings → Users.
expected: All three actions succeed without any permission errors.

title: Admin — Settings shows both Consultants and Users panels
steps: Log in as admin. Navigate to Settings.
expected: Both 'Consultants' and 'Users' are visible in the Settings left nav. Consultants panel is selected by default.

title: Admin — billing rates visible in consultant profile
steps: Log in as admin. Open any consultant profile in Settings → Consultants.
expected: Bill Rate and Cost Rate fields are visible and show numeric values.

title: Admin — sandbox reset available in Settings
steps: Log in as admin on a non-production sandbox tenant. Navigate to Settings. Look for a Sandbox panel or Reset option.
expected: A Sandbox or Reset panel is visible in Settings. A reset button or option is present.

title: Resource Manager — can edit heatmap and consultant data
steps: Log in as resource manager. Edit a heatmap cell. Open a consultant profile and change their location. Save both.
expected: Both edits succeed. RM has full data-edit access.

title: Resource Manager — sees Consultants but NOT Users in Settings
steps: Log in as resource manager. Navigate to Settings.
expected: Consultants panel is visible and functional. No 'Users' nav item appears in the Settings left nav.

title: Resource Manager — billing rates visible
steps: Log in as resource manager. Open a consultant profile in Settings → Consultants.
expected: Bill Rate and Cost Rate fields are visible.

title: Resource Manager — cannot close needs or bulk assign
steps: Log in as resource manager. Go to Open Needs. Confirm Close and Assign buttons are present and functional.
expected: Close and Assign buttons are visible and work. (RM has full data access on needs.)

title: Project Manager — heatmap is read-only
steps: Log in as project manager. Go to Resource Allocation. Click a week cell.
expected: Cell is not editable. No input field appears. Quick Fill bar is not shown.

title: Project Manager — can create and edit needs
steps: Log in as project manager. Go to Open Needs. Click Add Need and create a need. Then edit it.
expected: Create and edit both succeed without permission errors.

title: Project Manager — Close and Assign buttons not shown
steps: Log in as project manager. Go to Open Needs. Observe the action buttons on any need row.
expected: Close and Assign (bulk assign) buttons are either hidden or disabled. PM cannot close or assign needs.

title: Project Manager — billing rates not visible
steps: Log in as project manager. Click a consultant name in the heatmap to view their profile.
expected: Billing rate field is not visible in the consultant profile modal.

title: Project Manager — Settings tab is not visible
steps: Log in as project manager. Look at the sidebar navigation.
expected: Settings tab is not present in the sidebar.

title: Executive — read-only dashboard and heatmap
steps: Log in as executive. View the Overview and Resource Allocation tabs. Try to click a heatmap cell.
expected: All data loads correctly. No edit controls appear. Heatmap cells are not clickable.

title: Executive — can access Ask Claude
steps: Log in as executive. Click the Ask Claude tab. Type a question and submit.
expected: Ask Claude tab is visible. Question returns a valid response.

title: Executive — Settings tab not visible
steps: Log in as executive. Look at the sidebar navigation.
expected: Settings tab is not present in the sidebar.

title: Executive — billing rates not visible
steps: Log in as executive. Navigate to the Open Needs tab and view a need. Also check the heatmap for any rate display.
expected: No billing rate values are shown anywhere in the UI.

title: Consultant — sees only own heatmap row
steps: Log in as a user with consultant role linked to a consultant record.
expected: Resource Allocation heatmap shows only that consultant's own row. No other consultants' rows are visible.

title: Consultant — billing rates not visible
steps: Log in as consultant. Navigate to the app and look for any rate displays.
expected: Billing rate values are not shown anywhere in the UI.

title: Consultant — Settings tab not visible
steps: Log in as consultant. Look at the sidebar navigation.
expected: Settings tab is not present in the sidebar.

title: Consultant — can access Ask Claude
steps: Log in as consultant. Click the Ask Claude tab. Type a question and submit.
expected: Ask Claude tab is visible and accessible. A response is returned.

title: Consultant — heatmap row shows own projects only
steps: Log in as consultant. Expand your heatmap row.
expected: Sub-rows show only your own project assignments. No other consultants' project rows are shown.

title: Keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+1 through Ctrl+5 switch tabs
steps: Press Ctrl+1, then Ctrl+2, Ctrl+3, Ctrl+4, and Ctrl+5 in sequence.
expected: Each shortcut activates the corresponding tab: Overview, Resource Allocation, Open Needs, Ask Claude, Settings.

title: Keyboard shortcut ? shows help overlay
steps: Press the ? key while on any tab.
expected: A keyboard shortcut help overlay or modal appears listing all available shortcuts. It can be dismissed.

title: Esc closes open modals
steps: Open any modal (e.g. Add Need, Edit Consultant). Press Escape.
expected: The modal closes. No unsaved changes are applied (or a discard prompt appears if changes were made).

title: Alt+R refreshes data without full page reload
steps: While on any main tab, press Alt+R.
expected: App data reloads (spinner may appear briefly). The browser does not perform a full page reload.

title: Ctrl+B toggles sidebar collapse and expand
steps: Press Ctrl+B once to collapse the sidebar. Press Ctrl+B again to expand it.
expected: Sidebar collapses to icon-only or hidden state on first press. Expands back to full width on second press.

title: Settings tab defaults to Consultants panel on open
steps: Click Settings in the sidebar (or press Ctrl+5).
expected: The Consultants sub-panel is shown by default. The Consultants item in the Settings left nav is highlighted.

title: Settings panel selection persists across tab switches
steps: Go to Settings → Users. Switch to a different tab (e.g. Overview). Return to Settings.
expected: The Users panel is still selected when returning to Settings (selection is preserved, not reset to Consultants).

title: SSE auto-refresh — heatmap updates after consultant change
steps: Open the app in two browser windows as admin. In window 1, make a change to a consultant's assignment (edit a heatmap cell). Observe window 2.
expected: Window 2 receives a live update via SSE and refreshes the heatmap to show the change without requiring a manual page reload.

title: Dashboard shows only current tenant's data
steps: Log in as admin of one tenant. Note the KPI values and consultant names. Log out. Log in as admin of a different sandbox tenant.
expected: KPI values differ between tenants. The consultants visible belong only to the current tenant. No cross-tenant data appears.

title: Heatmap shows only current tenant's employees
steps: Log in as admin on one tenant. Note heatmap consultant names. Log in on a second tenant and compare.
expected: Each tenant sees only their own set of consultants. No consultant from another tenant appears.

title: Ask Claude references only current tenant's data
steps: Log in as admin of tenant A. Ask Claude: 'List all consultants.' Log in as admin of tenant B and ask the same.
expected: Each response contains only consultants from the respective tenant. No names from tenant A appear in tenant B's response.

title: Open Needs shows only current tenant's needs
steps: Log in as admin of one tenant. Note the open needs. Log out and log in to a different tenant.
expected: Each tenant has their own independent set of open needs. Needs from one tenant do not appear under another.

title: Candidate matching shows only current tenant's consultants
steps: Log in as admin. Open candidates for any open need.
expected: Only consultants from the current tenant appear as candidates. No consultants from other tenants are listed.

title: Admin can reset sandbox on non-production tenant
steps: Log in as admin on a non-production sandbox tenant (e.g. Acme Corp, BigCo Inc, or Summit LLC). Navigate to Settings and find the Sandbox Reset option. Trigger the reset.
expected: Reset completes. Data is wiped and reseeded with fresh synthetic data. Dashboard KPIs reload with the new data.

title: Sandbox reset rejected for production tenant
steps: Log in as admin on the production tenant (Meridian Consulting). Navigate to Settings and attempt to trigger a sandbox reset.
expected: Reset is rejected with an error message. No data is modified.

title: Non-admin roles cannot see Sandbox Reset option
steps: Log in as resource manager. Navigate to Settings → Consultants.
expected: No Sandbox Reset panel or reset button is visible anywhere in Settings.

title: After reset, heatmap shows fresh synthetic assignments
steps: On a sandbox tenant, trigger a reset. After completion, navigate to Resource Allocation.
expected: The heatmap loads with a fresh set of synthetic consultants and assignments. Previously modified cells no longer show the old values.

title: After reset, Open Needs repopulates with synthetic needs
steps: On a sandbox tenant, trigger a reset. After completion, navigate to Open Needs.
expected: The needs list shows a fresh set of synthetic open needs. Any previously created or closed needs are gone.
